macros_note: Per-serving estimates calculated from listed ingredients.

recipes.title: High Protein Overnight Oats
steps: Combine the oats, chia seeds, protein powder, cinnamon, Greek yoghurt, milk, honey and vanilla in a bowl or jar.
Stir until well combined.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, stir again and add your favourite toppings before serving.
recipes.note: The original didn't list amounts — these are sensible starting quantities. Adjust the milk/honey to taste.

recipes.title: Baked Blueberry Muffin Protein Bowl
steps: Mash the banana in a bowl.
Crack the egg into the bowl with the mashed banana.
Add the Greek yoghurt and vanilla essence to the mixture.
Add the protein powder, oats and baking powder to the bowl.
Pour in the milk and mix everything together thoroughly.
Fold in the blueberries and optional white chocolate chips.
Transfer the mixture to an oven-proof dish.
Bake in the oven at 180°C for about 30 minutes.

recipes.title: High Protein Banana Bread Baked Oats

items: 150g bananas (heated with a little brown sugar)

steps: Mix the oats, banana, protein, milk, baking powder, salt and yoghurt into a batter and pour into an oven dish.
Top with the brown-sugar bananas, walnuts and chocolate chips.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 30 minutes.

recipes.title: Cottage Cheese Protein Pancakes
steps: In a large bowl whisk together the cottage cheese, eggs, milk, vanilla extract, maple syrup and melted butter until smooth.
In a separate bowl mix the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and the whey protein (if using).
Gently fold the dry ingredients into the wet mix — a few lumps are fine.
Carefully fold in the blueberries.
Heat a pan over medium-low heat with a little butter.
Pour roughly a quarter of the mix per pancake.
Cook until bubbles form and the edges set, then flip and cook for another 1 to 2 minutes.
Stack them up, add butter, drizzle maple syrup and enjoy.
recipes.note: Macros are without added protein powder. Step 2 mentions whey — add a 30g scoop if you want, it'll push protein up ~20g per serving.

recipes.title: Protein Breakfast Boats
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
Mix everything except the boats and cheese.
Fill the boats evenly.
Top with the cheese.
Bake for 20–25 minutes.

recipes.title: High Protein Crispy Honey Garlic Chicken Fried Rice

items: 1.05kg diced boneless, skinless chicken breast (raw weight)
100g potato starch (roughly 40g sticks to the chicken)

steps: Add the salt reduced soy sauce to your chicken with the garlic, black pepper and onion powder, then mix well.
Add it to a Ziploc bag with the potato starch and mix until the chicken's fully coated.
Spray with light cooking oil and air fry until golden and crispy.
To a pan on medium heat add the frozen mixed vegetables followed by the mixed eggs.
Let it cook through and mix well.
Add the white part of the spring onion followed by the day-old cooked white rice.
Add a little soy sauce, mix well, then add the green part of the spring onion and mix to combine.
For the sauce: to a pan on medium heat add the garlic, equal parts water and soy sauce, ketchup, rice vinegar and the cornflour slurry to thicken. Take off the heat, add the honey and mix once more.
Coat the chicken in the sauce, divide everything into seven servings and enjoy.

recipes.title: One Tray Red Thai Chicken Rice Bake
steps: Add the rice, chicken, broccoli, peppers and green beans to a large baking dish.
Add the curry paste, ginger, garlic and seasonings.
Pour over the coconut milk and enough water to cover the rice.
Cover tightly with foil.
Bake at 190°C for 45 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for a further 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and chilli if using.
Shove it in your face.

recipes.title: Cheesy Beef Lasagna Rolls

ingredient_groups.heading: To Assemble
items: 150g low fat shredded mozzarella, divided (15g inside & 15g on top of each roll)

steps: Season and cook the ground beef on a nonstick pan over medium heat for ~6 minutes until browned.
Mix in the cottage cheese and tomato paste.
On a plate, place the low-carb tortilla, spread the sauce on the tortilla, place 1/6 of the total lasagna filling on the tortilla, and top off with 15g of cheese.
Spray with no-calorie oil spray and place seam-side down on an air fryer or oven tray, all rolls packed side by side. Top with 15g cheese and more sauce.
Air fry or bake for 10 minutes at 400°F (205°C).
Top with parsley. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Cheesy Chicken Tacos
steps: Slice your chicken thighs into thin strips and season with 2/3 of the taco seasoning, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Fry the chicken until cooked through and slightly crispy.
Dice your onion and pepper and fry them in the same pan.
Add the sweetcorn and black beans, then chuck in the rest of the taco seasoning.
Add the chicken back in and mix everything together.
Spray one side of each mini tortilla with oil.
Fill each tortilla with the taco mix, fold in half and stack upright in a baking dish.
Sprinkle each taco with mozzarella.
Bake at 180°C for 12 to 15 mins until golden and crispy.
Top with Greek yoghurt, salsa and coriander.

recipes.title: Garlic Parmesan Chicken Tenders
steps: Crush the cornflakes into crumbs. Not dust. You still want a bit of crunch.
Season the chicken with salt, pepper, paprika and coriander if using.
Dip the chicken into the egg, then coat in the crushed cornflakes.
Air fry at 180°C for around 10 minutes until cooked through and crispy.
Mix the melted butter, garlic purée and parmesan together.
Toss the cooked chicken in the garlic parmesan butter.
recipes.note: The 40g butter does most of the fat here — halve it and you'll knock ~8g fat off per serving without losing much.

recipes.title: High Protein Crispy Chicken Shawarma

ingredient_groups.heading: Shawarma Chicken
items: 900g boneless skinless chicken thighs (fat trimmed off)

ingredient_groups.heading: Garlic Sauce
items: 1 tsp garlic powder or minced garlic

ingredient_groups.heading: Other
items: 4 Lebanese wraps (60g each), carefully separated into two layers

steps: Slice the chicken thighs into strips.
Add the yoghurt, lemon juice, garlic, shawarma seasoning and tomato paste. Mix well to fully coat the chicken.
Marinate until ready to cook.
For the garlic sauce, combine all the ingredients in a bowl, mix well and set aside.
Spray a baking tray, add the marinated chicken and bake at 200°C for 20–25 minutes.
Broil/grill on high afterward (3–5 minutes) to achieve a golden char.
For the crispy coating, mix the oil, tomato paste, salt and chilli flakes until combined.
Separate a wrap into two. Spread a generous amount of garlic sauce onto it, add the cooked shawarma and sliced pickles, then roll tightly.
Place the wraps into a pan over medium heat with the shawarma coating. Baste the outside and roll until golden and crispy on all sides.
Serve hot and crispy and enjoy.

recipes.title: Beef Fajita Flatbreads
steps: Heat ½ tbsp olive oil in a pan, add the beef mince and break it apart. Cook until no longer pink. Add all the seasonings and chopped tomatoes, stir well and cook 4–5 minutes until the tomatoes soften. Remove the beef and set aside.

In the same pan add ½ tbsp olive oil, the sliced onion and pepper and cook 3–4 minutes over high heat. Add the chopped garlic, stir, then return the beef mixture with the peppers and mix well. Turn off the heat.
Place the flatbreads in a roasting tray. Top with the beef mixture, grated cheese and some sliced chilli. Bake at 180°C for 6–7 minutes until the cheese has melted.
Combine the chipotle sauce ingredients in a small bowl and drizzle over the flatbreads.
Garnish with chopped parsley / coriander.

recipes.title: Creamy Paprika Chicken
steps: Add the chopped chicken breast to a bowl and sprinkle over the smoked paprika, paprika and mixed herbs. Season with salt and pepper, then toss well to coat evenly.

Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook for 3–4 minutes until lightly golden on the outside (it doesn't need to be fully cooked through). Remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, reduce the heat slightly and add the chopped onion. Cook for 4–5 minutes until softened, then stir in the garlic and cook for another 30 seconds.
Increase the heat to high and pour in the white wine, scraping up any bits from the bottom of the pan. Let it simmer and reduce by about half.

Add the chicken stock, Dijon mustard and tomato purée. Stir well to combine, then return the chicken (and any juices) to the pan. Simmer gently for 6–8 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir through the single cream and let it heat through for 1–2 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve immediately with pasta, rice or potatoes.
recipes.note: Macros are for the chicken and sauce only — add your pasta, rice or potatoes on top.
